generatedAtMilestone: M7 complete (M5 Toy C compiler, M6 debugger, M7 workspace UI all merged)
purpose: Context handover so a fresh session can continue building NovaOS milestone-by-milestone without re-deriving prior decisions. Read this first, then docs/specs and docs/adr.

project.name: NovaOS
project.oneLiner: A browser-based, deterministic operating-systems laboratory: custom VM/CPU, microkernel, scheduler, virtual filesystem, shell/terminal, NovaASM assembler, (upcoming) Toy C compiler, debugger with time-travel replay, and a premium DevTools-style UI.
project.specs: docs/specs/ (10 authoritative markdown docs, numbered 01-10). Read the relevant one before each milestone.
project.adrs: docs/adr/ (0001 foundation+tooling, 0002 events package + canonical event shape, 0003 kernel/runtime responsibilities + kernel->cpu dep).
project.originalGoal: Build M0 through v1.0 (M10), one milestone per turn, production quality, main always green.

git.head: 47c1cfa
git.headMessage: feat(web): Milestone 7 — browser workspace UI (Vite React SPA) (#5)
git.workflow: Each milestone: branch off main, implement+test, PR, wait for CI green, squash-merge, pull main. Merged PRs #3 (M5), #4 (M6), #5 (M7).

currentState.adrsAdded: 0004 (ISA extension + calling convention for Toy C), 0005 (workspace UI = Vite React SPA not Next.js)
milestonesComplete: M5 Toy C compiler (@novaos/compiler: lexer/parser/sema/IR/optimize/codegen/source-map; ISA extended in cpu+assembler per ADR-0004; .c wired into runner; hello.c seeded)
M6 debugger (@novaos/debugger: run control, stepping into/over/out, 5 breakpoint kinds, safe watch evaluator, call-stack via BP chain, timeline, time-travel replay)
M7 workspace UI (apps/web Vite+React SPA: editor, compile inspector, run output, live debugger panel; Playwright e2e drives built SPA)
currentState.nextMilestone: M8 agent orchestration (spec 08); then M9 testing/devops (spec 09); then M10 public v1.0 launch (spec 10). User wants M8-M10 done overnight.
currentState.gates: pnpm validate is green (format:check, lint, typecheck, test, build, check:arch all pass).
currentState.buildArtifacts: tsup dist/ is gitignored; tests/build run from TS source via vitest/tsc.

howToWork.process: User says 'proceed with Milestone N'. Implement ONLY that milestone, fully, with real code + tests, keeping every gate green. Then report: files changed, APIs introduced, events introduced, tests added, commands run, deviations, next milestone.
howToWork.doNotFabricate: Never ship placeholder/stub implementations and call them done. Never claim output that wasn't actually produced. If a spec is ambiguous, make a documented engineering decision (add an ADR if architectural) and continue.
howToWork.commitPolicy: Commit/push ONLY when the user asks. The user has been committing between turns (e.g. 'm2-m4'). Do NOT auto-commit; offer a message in the report.
howToWork.verifyEachMilestone: After writing code: run pnpm typecheck, pnpm test, pnpm lint, pnpm build, pnpm check:arch, pnpm format then pnpm validate. Also run the milestone's acceptance demo via tsx and paste the real transcript.

toolchain.taskRunner: Turborepo (turbo.json: only 'build' + 'typecheck' tasks; lint/test run centrally at root)
toolchain.test: Vitest (root vitest.config.ts; include packages/*/src/**/*.test.ts + tests/unit/**). Playwright skeleton in tests/e2e (page-free, no browser needed yet).
toolchain.lint: ESLint flat config (eslint.config.mjs) + typescript-eslint recommended + eslint-config-prettier. Run as `eslint .` at root. max-warnings 0.
toolchain.format: Prettier (.prettierrc.json: singleQuote, semi, trailingComma all, printWidth 100, 2-space). .prettierignore excludes docs/specs.
toolchain.typecheck: Single root `tsc --noEmit -p tsconfig.json` over the whole repo (no project references). tsconfig.base.json is strict + noUncheckedIndexedAccess + verbatimModuleSyntax + isolatedModules. exactOptionalPropertyTypes is OFF (so assigning undefined to optional fields is allowed).
toolchain.moduleResolution: Bundler; workspace packages resolve via pnpm symlinks + each package.json main/types -> ./src/index.ts. No tsconfig path aliases.
toolchain.archCheck: scripts/check-architecture.ts (run via tsx) enforces: only-root-imports (no @novaos/x/internal), declared-dependency, shared-has-no-workspace-deps, ui-is-domain-free, no-UI-in-domain, determinism (no Math.random/Date.now in deterministic pkgs), no eval, acyclic graph. ALL @novaos imports (incl. in tests) must be declared in that package's package.json deps/devDeps.

commands.demoViaTsx: pnpm --filter @novaos/<pkg> exec tsx -e "(async()=>{ ... dynamic import('./src/index.ts') and '@novaos/*' ...})()"

invariants: Simulator/domain core owns truth; UI only consumes serializable snapshots + typed events. UI never owns simulation truth.
Dependency direction strictly downward; package graph acyclic (enforced by check:arch).
Domain packages must NOT import React/DOM/Monaco/Next/Tailwind/Zustand. @novaos/ui must NOT import any domain package.
Every package exports only via src/index.ts. No deep imports.
Deterministic: no Date.now()/Math.random() in domain logic. Use injected SimulationClock (shared) + seeded DeterministicRandom (shared).
All meaningful state changes emit typed, serializable DomainEvents (source-tagged). Snapshots are serializable + versioned.
No eval/new Function for user programs; assembly/Toy C compile to NovaOS bytecode executed only by the VM interpreter.
Strict TS, no implicit any, no untyped public APIs, branded types for ids/addresses, discriminated unions for events/diagnostics.

keyExports: EventBus/createEventBus (publish assigns id 'evt-N' + monotonic sequence; subscribe(matcher,listener); drain)
HANDLERS (pure InstructionHandler per opcode; SYSCALL/HALT special-cased in step)
createRegisterFile/RegisterFileSnapshot {r0..r7,pc,sp,bp,ir,flags}, FlagsRegister {zero,negative,carry,overflow,interruptEnabled,exception}, INITIAL_FLAGS, GPR_NAMES, gprNameFromIndex, RegisterName
createCpu()->Cpu (reset/getRegisters/setRegister/getSnapshot/restoreSnapshot/step(ctx)); CpuStepResult {status:'ok'|'halted'|'fault', cycles, instruction, fault, syscall?}
@novaos/cpu.notes: MOV is immediate-only (no register-to-register MOV). No JMP/CMP/LOAD/STORE yet. PRINT opcode prints a register directly (bare-VM convenience); kernel print is SYSCALL 0.

@novaos/memory.notes: No memory events / permission-enforced access yet (deferred). Segment serialization not in MemorySnapshot yet.

@novaos/scheduler.notes: Operates on minimal SchedulableProcess view (no PCB dep). Priority/SJF/Lottery deferred. Kernel emits scheduler.* events.

@novaos/kernel.purpose: Boot, process table, syscalls, interrupts, context switch.
@novaos/kernel.notes: Kernel=policy, simulator=run loop. init(pid1)+shell(pid2) created as parked 'new' placeholders (no executable, not scheduled). print syscall reads R0; exit reads R0 as code.

@novaos/filesystem.notes: Owner read/write enforced; single-user 'student'. Symlinks/devices typed but not seeded. Recursive dir copy not supported. Real IndexedDB adapter deferred to apps/web.

keyExports: ProgramRunner + CompileReport/RunReport/RunnerDiagnostic (compile/run; backed by simulator runner)
@novaos/shell.notes: Shell is decoupled from kernel/assembler via injected SystemInspector + ProgramRunner. Pipes/redirects/semicolons lexed but rejected. Execution is synchronous.

@novaos/terminal.notes: Acceptance tests boot a real kernel and wire its snapshots into a SystemInspector to prove the shell reads real kernel state.

@novaos/assembler.notes: Two-pass. entryPoint = byte offset of first .global symbol. Memory/label operands parsed but rejected (no LOAD/STORE/JMP). Deterministic encoding (golden-tested).

keyExports: createProgramRunner()->ProgramRunner (compile/run; assemble + boot fresh NovaRuntime + spawn + run + capture output)
@novaos/simulator.notes: ProgramRunner shape duplicated structurally with shell's ProgramRunner to avoid simulator->shell cycle.

@novaos/compiler.purpose: Toy C compiler — IMPLEMENT IN M5.

@novaos/ui.purpose: Design system — IMPLEMENT IN M7. Must stay domain-free.

apps/web.status: STUB (placeholder, NOT a Next.js app yet)
apps/web.purpose: Browser workspace UI — IMPLEMENT IN M7 (Next.js+React+Monaco+Tailwind+Zustand). Only place allowed to bridge React with domain types.

isaReference.instructionFormat: Fixed 32-bit word: [OPCODE(8) | A(8) | B(8) | C(8)]. Stored little-endian. encodeInstruction = ((op<<24)|(a<<16)|(b<<8)|c)>>>0.

operandMapping.MOV: MOV Rd, imm8 -> a=d,b=imm (immediate-only; NO reg-reg MOV)

syscalls.0: print: writes R0 as decimal line to OutputSink, returns 0 in R0
syscalls.4: exit: terminates current process with code = R0

isaReference.registers: 8 GPR r0-r7 + pc/sp/bp/ir + flags. Stored unsigned 32-bit.
isaReference.missingFeaturesToAddLater: register-to-register MOV, CMP, JMP/conditional branches, LOAD/STORE memory ops, CALL/RET/PUSH/POP stack ops. M5 codegen will likely NEED these (functions/if/while). DECIDE in M5 whether to extend the ISA (cpu+assembler) before/with the compiler.

keyDecisions: ADR-0002: events is its own package (@novaos/events) depending only on shared. Canonical DomainEvent uses tick:SimTime (reconciles spec's tick vs timestamp). Bus assigns id ('evt-N') + monotonic sequence on publish.

ADR-0003: kernel=policy, simulator=run loop. SYSCALL opcode + SyscallTrap (defined in cpu, implemented by kernel). kernel depends on cpu for RegisterFileSnapshot + trap types (acyclic). Kernel saves/restores registers via injected RegisterPort. Scheduler stays kernel-free via SchedulableProcess view. init/shell parked as 'new'.
print syscall (and PRINT opcode) read/print R0 per VM spec section 22 and M1/M2 ABI. M2 demo uses R0. (See deviations below re: M4 acceptance file.)

Shell decoupled from kernel and assembler via injected SystemInspector and ProgramRunner interfaces (defined in shell/context.ts; impls live in the runtime). createStaticSystemInspector for standalone/tests.
Single root tsc for typecheck (no project references). tsup JS-only builds. exactOptionalPropertyTypes OFF.
deviationsAndGotchas: M4 print/R2 vs R0: The VM spec's toolchain demo file does `ADD R2,R0,R1` then `SYSCALL 0` expecting 15, but print reads R0 and there is NO register-to-register MOV in the ISA, so that exact file would print 5. The runnable hello.asm targets R0 (`ADD R0, R0, R1`) to print 15; the assembler golden test still assembles the verbatim `ADD R2` file correctly. Documented deviation.

TOOLING GREMLIN: writing a literal control/space char inside a regex char class via the Write/Edit tools can corrupt it (a typed space became a NUL byte once). Avoid control-char regex literals; use charCodeAt scans. ESLint also forbids control chars in regex (no-control-regex). See filesystem/src/path.ts hasInvalidChars for the pattern.
BSD grep on macOS treats \| literally (not alternation). Use `grep -E` for alternation.

Prettier reformats hand-written files; ALWAYS run `pnpm format` before `pnpm format:check`/validate, or format:check fails. Linter/formatter edits files between turns (expect 'file modified' notices).
Stub package files were created by a shell script in M0; you must Read them before Write/Edit (harness requirement).
noUncheckedIndexedAccess is ON: array/tuple index access returns T|undefined; guard or use charAt/?? .
When adding a package or cross-package import, update that package.json deps/devDeps or check:arch fails (it validates declared deps for ALL imports incl. tests).

nextMilestone.title: Toy C Compiler
deliverables: semantic analyzer + symbol table + type checker
optimizations: constant folding, dead code elimination, copy propagation
source maps connecting Toy C -> assembly/bytecode
nextMilestone.criticalDesignNote: The current ISA lacks reg-reg MOV, CMP, branches (JMP/JE/JNE/JLT/JGT), and stack ops (CALL/RET/PUSH/POP) that real codegen for if/while/functions needs. BEFORE/WITH the compiler you will likely need to EXTEND the ISA in @novaos/cpu (new opcodes + handlers + tests) AND @novaos/assembler (specs + label-ref operands for branch targets, which are already parsed but currently rejected). This is the biggest M5 design decision — extend cpu+assembler first, keep all existing tests green, then build the compiler on top. Document any ISA change in an ADR.
nextMilestone.wiring: Extend @novaos/simulator createProgramRunner to dispatch .c files to the compiler (compile -> NovaASM -> assemble -> bytecode -> run). The shell compile/run commands already dispatch by extension via the runner. The ProgramRunner.compile/run already exist; add a '.c' branch.
nextMilestone.callingConvention: Spec 06 section 15: R0 return value; first args in R0-R3 else stack; BP/SP frames; prologue PUSH BP; MOV BP,SP; epilogue MOV SP,BP; POP BP; RET. Adjust to actual ISA; document in ADR if different.
nextMilestone.verify: pnpm validate green; tsx demo running `compile hello.c` then `run hello.c` -> 15.

remainingMilestones.M6: Debugger, Timeline, Replay (@novaos/debugger). Session state machine; run/pause/continue/stop/restart; step instruction/source/into/over/out; line+instruction breakpoints; register/memory watches + safe watch parser; call stack; debugger snapshots+events; event timeline + correlation grouping; snapshot-interval + deterministic replay engine (rewind/forward/jump); source-map integration (line<->address). Uses cpu.getSnapshot/restoreSnapshot + the event recorder + assembler/compiler source maps. Specs 03/04/06/09/10.
remainingMilestones.M7: Full Workspace UI (apps/web as real Next.js+React+Monaco+Tailwind+shadcn+Zustand; @novaos/ui design system). BIG/multi-turn, different toolchain. Design tokens + primitives; app shell/top bar/resizable panels/layout persistence; command palette; settings; dark/light/high-contrast; reduced motion. Feature UI consuming REAL snapshots/events: file explorer, Monaco editor, terminal UI, register/flags viewer, process table, scheduler viz, kernel dashboard, virtualized memory grid, stack/heap viewers, debugger panels, timeline UI, compiler inspector. E2E (Playwright) flagship flow + a11y smoke. NO domain truth in React state. Will need a worker/runtime-client to drive the simulator. Specs 07/08/09/10.
remainingMilestones.M8: Tutorials, Examples, Documentation. Tutorial engine+overlay+step model+validation; 8 tutorials; example gallery + metadata under /usr/examples; ~12 example programs; full docs set (README/quickstart/guides/glossary/known-limitations). Education+polish only, no new architecture. Specs 07/09/10.
remainingMilestones.M9: Release Candidate. Stabilization/bugfix/a11y/perf/docs. Full validation pass; hide unstable non-MVP behind flags; polish empty/error/loading states; verify deploy/reset/import-export; screenshots; release notes; go/no-go. Specs 09/10.
remainingMilestones.M10: Public v1.0 Launch. Final build/deploy, polished README, screenshots/GIFs, architecture diagrams, demo script, release notes, known limitations, contributing/issue/PR templates, license, GitHub polish, final validation report. Specs all.

flagshipDemo: open app -> boot -> open Toy C/asm example -> compile -> inspect compiler artifacts -> run as process -> terminal output -> inspect registers/memory/process table/scheduler/syscalls/timeline -> debug session -> breakpoint -> step -> watch registers/memory change -> rewind timeline.
resumePrompt: Read handover.json, then docs/specs/06-compiler-debugger-v2.md. Proceed with Milestone 5: implement the Toy C compiler in @novaos/compiler (lexer/parser/AST/semantic+types/IR/opt passes/codegen/source maps/compiler-inspector snapshots + golden tests), extend the ISA in @novaos/cpu + @novaos/assembler as needed for if/while/functions (document any ISA change as an ADR), wire .c into @novaos/simulator's createProgramRunner so `compile hello.c`/`run hello.c` work, keep main green (pnpm validate), and report in the standard format. The acceptance program must print 15.
